$notes: Semantic companion to recipe-lint's grammar check (#496 RC3). recipe-lint reads a driver's `case "$type"` arms to know which step types it ACCEPTS — but accepting a step type is not the same as PRODUCING its effect. The canonical trap: headless `opencode run` accepts a `send` step but stores a leading-slash text as a literal user message, so `send "/undo"` lints in-grammar yet elicits nothing. This manifest declares, per adapter, the step types whose effect the driver genuinely produces (`elicits`), plus whether slash commands must use a dedicated step type rather than send-text (`slash_requires_step_type`). `elicits` is normally a subset of the driver's case arms; a discovery-scaffolded driver (RC2) stubs every standard arm but only lists here the ones it truly elicits, so a recipe needing a stubbed-but-not-elicited primitive is caught as a semantic gap BEFORE a recording is attempted.

claudecode.notes: Full tmux-TUI driver; every dispatched step type is genuinely produced. `send|slash` is one arm, so a slash typed via send reaches the REPL.

pi.notes: tmux-TUI driver. No `keys` arm — the /tree picker (checkpoint-rewind) needs arrow-key navigation it cannot elicit; that cell is a driver_gap awaiting `keys` via extend-driver.

opencode.notes: Hybrid driver. Headless `opencode run` (send/wait_turn/sleep) ignores in-REPL slash commands — it stores a leading-slash text as a literal user message. The live-TUI path (run_live, #495) genuinely elicits slash/reset_session, but ONLY when the recipe uses those STEP TYPES; a bare send "/cmd" never reaches the REPL, so slash_requires_step_type is true.
